reviews.title: Không tương xứng giá tiền
reviews.text: Phòng ốc cũ kỹ từ đời tống, nội thất tủ kệ thì long sòng sọc lởm khởm. Giường năm thì bồng bềnh khó chịu, Đến cái cửa vào phòng cũng bị hư. Mình ở phòng fammily suite hơn 100m vuông. Wifi còn chậm rì ko thể vào được gì. Bỏ ra 8tr 1 đêm ở đây ko đáng tiền chút nào
reviews.response:
Kính gửi chị Xuân,

Chúng tôi chân thành cảm ơn chị đã chia sẻ phản hồi về kỳ lưu trú tại The IMPERIAL Hotel Vung Tau. Chúng tôi rất tiếc khi biết rằng phòng Family Suite và một số tiện nghi trong kỳ nghỉ của chị và gia đình chưa đạt được kỳ vọng. Quý Khách vui lòng liên hệ với với Quản Lý Tiền sảnh -anh Hải FOM qua email "fom@imperialhotel.vn" để chúng tôi có cơ hội được lắng nghe trải nghiệm chưa được kỳ vọng của quý khách.

Xin được ghi nhận ý kiến của Quý khách để chúng tôi rà soát và cải thiện chất lượng dịch vụ, nhằm đảm bảo tất cả khách lưu trú tại khách sạn đều có trải nghiệm thoải mái và xứng đáng với tiêu chuẩn 5 sao.

Chúng tôi hy vọng sẽ có cơ hội được đón tiếp chị trở lại và mang đến một trải nghiệm tốt hơn trong tương lai.

Trân trọng,
Ban Quản Lý Khách Sạn
The IMPERIAL Hotel Vung Tau

reviews.title: Avoid This Place! Disgusting!
reviews.text: This place is not worth the premium price you’ll be paying. This is not near a 5-star hotel in any way. This hotel hides behind their title of the first 5-star Hotel in Vung Tau. I stayed here for 3 days and 2 nights and regretted every little bit of it. The room we stayed in had a horrible smell of mold and urine. Their way of resolving this issue was sending someone to spray essence oil around  the room to hide the smell. That is a serious hygiene issue.  We even brought this matter to the manager, lady with bob cut hair, and she quietly disappeared after offering us a fruit plate and spa voucher. I denied their offer and never saw the manager again. Such terrible management. Beware this place is infested with cockroaches. We had a room change after two attempts calling the front desk regarding the horrible urine smell and odor. In the morning after, a cockroach was crawling on my wife’s back… We complained to the manager and she just had housekeeping make up our room. Luckily we booked through Agoda and explained the entire ordeal. Agoda shared with is what The Imperial Hotel had responded and the Hotel changed the story completely about the cockroach. They never even mentioned anything about the urine odor in the initial room we stayed in. Agoda was willing to assist us but the Hotel refused in offering any proper apology. Their offer was a plate of two apples and an orange, which we can get during breakfast as much as we want. Think very carefully if you’re planning on paying the premium price staying here. The pictures you see online is when the Hotel was first built. Management never upkeep this hotel and now it’s like an old run-down cheap hotel blanket with some fancy decorations.

reviews.title: Never come back
reviews.text:
1. it was not a busy day (Sunday night) and I didn't see many guests. I requested early check-in one week in advance due to elders in the family and they kept us waiting for 1 hour in the lobby (we arrived at 2 PM)
2. When I finally got the room's key (one key for a family room of 4), no one walked us on. Just handed over the key and that's it. We had to ask for the way and then walked by ourselves (no one walked us after being asked)
3. The bathrobes were unwashed. The smell of other people's sweat is still 100%
4. Two of the towels smell like...
5. Wifi is terrible
6. They call it's family room but the second bedroom is the living room with a bed. The toilet does not have any electronic plug whatsoever, just the sink. Two bedrooms (actually 1 bedroom and 1 living room with a bed) but we had to share the same bathroom to take a shower. And whoever stays in the 2nd bedroom (living room) must walk through the actual bedroom bed to enter the bathroom...
7. The electronic was a nightmare. When we turned off the light in the actual bedroom, the 2nd bedroom (living room) lights were turned off completely including the very small and unusable toilet. If a person who lives in the living room wants to go to the toilet and turn on the toilet light , all lights are turned on including lights in the actual bedroom. I tested all and there is no other way
reviews.response:

I have noted down and shared your feedback to Head of Departments concern, we hope you choose to stay with us again so we can show you our true level of service.
Thanks and best regards 
BeBe Yen Nguyen (Ms.)

reviews.title: Horrible accommodation, not worth the price
reviews.text:
⁃	terrible service at receptionist, super long wait w/ only 2 working lines (but there were plenty of employees + the manager just hanging in the backroom) checking in + out ~20-30min wait time
	⁃	pools MUCH smaller than in picture 
	⁃	claims that restaurants within hotel gets 15% discount however it was not applied when we paid our bill at la sirena
	⁃	room keycard doesnt work - have to try 6-8 times avg before getting door to open. door also randomly beeps throughout the day/night bc it cannot close properly (broken/old hinges???)
	⁃	no hot water. boiler is only 70L so it will randomly turn into freezing cold water on you while you’re showering. all rooms are the same (according the the maintenance man who came to check for us). But sink water will randomly turn into boiling hot water when you wash your hands
	⁃	lots of outlets around the room but only 40% of them work 
	⁃	bathtub leaks so the floor is always flooded after you take a shower
	⁃	insists on giving us the bill after every drink we ordered even though we are not done ordering
	⁃	claims that breakfast ends at 10:30 but all the food were already taken away at 10am
        -       loud elevator music playing in the hallway starting from 7am every morning

If your budget is ~$200, please do yourself a favor and book another hotel. Disappointed and won’t be coming back!

reviews.title: Poor customer services for agoda booking
reviews.text:
The management here is utterly stupid. There was a glitch in the agoda booking and they acknowledge it but still reply with, "You have a non-refundable booking by agoda and we cannot make any adjustments." 

The booking incorrectly indicated that it an extra bed is needed and it was not. Through multiple calls, email and in-person explanation, the hotel still stood to the position that it's an agoda problem and they don't care. 

So henceforth, I'm never staying at The Imperial Hotel Danang again because the hotel management and policies are much too ridiculous.  

Aside from this, the hotel is run down and proper maintenance is not kept up to standard. The breakfast that I had to pay extra for is equal to a 2 star rated hotel much less a 4 star hotel. 

One of my worse hotel experience over a ten year span. 

Thank goodness I won't ever have to stay here again.
reviews.response:
Dear Ryan,
From the outset we would like to apologize for the inconvenience caused. After receiving your feedback we did investigation on all the points you mentioned. 
Although we understand your frustration about not being able to get refund for extra bed,please be informed that Extra bed is indicated clearly  “ Children 6 years and older must use and extra bed…” therefore the choice of extra bed was made by guest .Hotel or Agoda did not provide wrong information in your case. Cancellation policy of our hotel is in line with what is practiced by other hotels worldwide for non refundable type as a frequent traveler you are already are of so we hope you understand. 
Regarding about hotel maintenance and breakfast, we truly appreciate your constructive feedback, we will share with our team especially to our new chef who commits to make difference on culinary experience to our guest.
Ryan, thank you again for your valuable feedback and understanding

Sincerely,
Hotel Management

Room was large but outdated and was not cleaned probably from 2-3 months before (carpet was full of hair, dusty, tooth paste or shaving foam on bathroom furniture). The breakfast restaurant was not clean (plates, cups and glasses were smelly as though they had not been washed from previous customers) and the food was terrible. We have had to check out earlier than expected because it was awful. We do not recommend other customers to stay there.

reviews.title: Too noisy at breakfast. Non smoking became smoking for some rude people
reviews.text:
The place is outdated. Breakfast was disappointing due to smoking. And the staff was poor. The food was old and not fresh.  
Breakfast is a time for quite but it was loud due to kids making too much noise.  We had a headache from it.  The staff let customer get away with smoking when the. Sign says no-smoking.  There should be a fine for smoking in restaurants but no one is doing anything about it. Very poor and we had to move away from that section.  Also the air conditioning was not working in some sections.  We would not be coming back and we would not recommend it to our family and friends.

reviews.title: A big disappointment
reviews.text: Extremely bad. Besides writing in the feedback section, I've also lodge a complaint direct to the hotel management. When I arrived, one of the rooms had wet carpet. I had taken 2 rooms and they only had rooms on another floor which I did not want due to the inconvenience. There wasn't even any offer to upgrade my room, etc. We tolerated that and the wet carpet took more than 1 day to dry. That night, I asked the receptionist for recommendations for dinner and the recommendation was Ganh Hao. Since I was travelling with young children, distance to the restaurant was important and she informed me that it will take about 7mins by taxi and she gave me a piece of paper with the restaurant name for the cab. The cab ride took 30mins to arrive even when the driver was driving very dangerously. The biggest problem happened when I checked out. I had informed the door man/concierge that I had booked a private transport at 1:30pm and to inform me when it arrived. That was at 1:10pm. I waited at the lobby and rechecked with the concierge at 1:30pm and they informed me to continue to wait. I had tried to contact the transport company but was not successful. At 1:50pm, I asked the receptionist to help if they can contact the transport company for me. That was then one of the receptionist said that there was a transporter waiting since 1pm. That was then I blew up and complained to the management of the inefficiencies of his staff. I paid for a 5star hotel but I got a 1star service. Hotel Imperial Vung Tau needs to buck up!!! Not recommended to anyone!

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Paid 1700 usd dollars for a family of 12. Was advise we couldn’t bring food or outside room to our family suites. Per security it’s their policy and procedure. When questioned the staff back changed his story to no seafood. Was not please on how they treated my family members who are residents of Vietnam.  Staff will treat foreigner better than the locals.

reviews.title: No hot water,  not worth the money
reviews.text:
The surrounding business has been closed down. The area at night is fairly dark. The rain was pouring and the city was flooded after only. 5 mins. 

The first room was stink with carpet mold dew. The second room has no hot water. We wanted to change the room again but it really not worth the hassle of moving when we stayed for only 3 nights.  The business center was occupied by their sales team . We used the inside meeting but was told to leave or pay 2 mil per hour. They rather leave it unused than giving it an hour complimentary for the guests.  I don’t understand what is the point of having the business center for. We don’t have hair conditioner for the hotel. I came to ask about it and the receptionist asked me to go buy outside and there is a convenient store next door, she said.  The main turning door is messed up and you gotta push really hard.. The water in the pools is always cloudy;  Even after they pour chemicals in it. Overall , it’s really not worth the money we paid for.

Thanks and Best regards,
Hotel Management Team.

reviews.title: Definitely not a 5 star hotel.
reviews.text:
Check-in took so much time during weekend. Stuck in the sea of lousy tour-group people. I were travelling with infant, toddler and my grandpa. This definitely made all of us unconfortable.
Room are not worth the money - Smelly, balcony facing concrete wall, toilet cloged,  slippery bathroom floor,... which to name a few.
Pool were packed with people wearing full clothing. I am not kidding, expect to see jeans, denim shorts, tshirt but not swimwear in the pool. 
Gotta hold myself a bit 'cause I can keep telling about my bad experience there. Should choose the newly open Pullman instead.

reviews.title: More like a 3 star
reviews.text:
Through my work as F&B/travel writer for several top magazines, I get to visit many 4/5 star hotels and resorts in Vietnam,aiming to have a relaxing experience I booked a room for three nights at the Imperial Vung Tau, I could not be more wrong!
Trouble started around two seconds after I reached the reception area: three receptionists were free, so I started talking to one of them, asking her to check in. By the second or so it took me to get my passport, a woman started talking to her, and rather than asking the woman to wait or move one metre to the next receptionist, she ignored me and started handling her reservation.
So I moved to the next receptionist, handled her my booking, my passport, residence card, and my girlfriends id. Click, click, click and I was asked to go to the lobby. I asked three times why, until the time I became invisible and she started talking to my girlfriend. Apparently she needed 10 minutes to do the check in. I asked why ten minutes, to be told, ok one minute only. 
When asked when the room would be ready, it was about 12 already, she told me she did not know, but no later than 2pm and she would call me when ready. At 2.40 pm, with no call received, we decided to head back to the hotel. On arrival again, room not ready. When sked, No answer, I was ignored, until I decided to do  I raised my voice, so I then got some attention, and my room was ready in 5 minutes, or so was I told.
Bed was made, but not very clean, towels missing, soap and gel missing, you name it. Five stars? In the sky may be.
Regardless of children swimming pools, and clear usage norms, children will be in the adults pool, playing ball, water pistols, throwing water or the pistols themselves, bathing with street clothes,… anything unacceptable is allowed! 
This hotel is a nightmare,I would not stay there even if half price, The Imperial is frustrating, stressful and a real no-go place.

reviews.title: Do not book
reviews.text: Took more than 20 minutes to check in. Then we had to walk to the other side of the hotel. Hallways of hotel are not carpeted and in the morning staff roll loud trolleys over. No instruction with safebox so you have to call someone to come up and set it up. Also, there is a large fridge in the room which constantly makes noise as compressor kicks in. Not worth the money.

reviews.title: not worth it
reviews.text: As soon as we got there the staff took our luggage saying that they would deliver it to our room.  After checking in an hour later we still did not get our bag.  My husband went down to the lobby to get our bag and they staff had it open looking through my iPad.  We were furious that they had not remorse or thought that this was an innovation of privacy.  We will never stay there again.  Maybe a 2 or 3 star hotel...I'm not sure how it got more. Also, there is a open market behind that plays their music too loud all night long, so if you are looking for peace it is not the place.

reviews.title: Never a 5 Star
reviews.text:
I booked a De Luxe double room and got one in the newly opened building. According to stuff it just opened in May 2016. However, having traveled all over the world, never in my life I saw a room with such dirty windows. You even couldnt look out of the bathroom cz.the window was full of cement and dirt from construction works. This building has never been cleaned up. Inside the room, no minibar, no hotel guide book ( for customer information). The shower was leaking at the hot water faucet and two times I burned my feet with boiling water running down from the faucet. The pool at the beach was dirty and full of water bugs.
The overall ambience inside the hotel was more like a main train station, crowded and packed with families and kids crying and driving around the lobby on roller blades or use the public areas as gaming zone.
Bottom line: a total miss management of a 5 Star hotel.
The ony positive thing was the stuff. Friendly and supporting, tried their best.
Never ever again.

reviews.title: 4star hotel at most
reviews.text: Just an unpleasant experience from the time of checkin to checkout. Maybe its because we booked from agoda, we were told to wait until our room was available while literally everyone else was getting checked in. So after an hour of waiting at the bar and seeing everyone being checked in, we went back to the counter and asked if our room was ready. The lady with a poker face just gave us a simple NO. So i complained how come we are the only couple not being checked in and then she made 1 PHONE CALL and our room was ready right away. Well that says a lot about this hotel in my opinion. The building, facilites seemed very old and poorly maintained. The spa was actually good compared to the poor service we received downstairs. And i warn you guys do not get the breakfast. It is terrible. The best part of the hotel was the outdoor beach pool connected by the bridge. Dont worry if theres no sun because its a heated pool. Other staff were very nice though from the guy holding the main hotel door getting us a cab to the other lady working the floor who actually smiled. And guess what? The same unpleasant lady took care of our check out. No bye, no thank you. She gave us the bill for the spa and all she said was CREDIT CARD. The hotel really needs to educate the service of some of its employees.

reviews.title: Outside is beautiful, Inside is very inconvenient
reviews.text: This hotel is built and owned by an Vietnam which makes the "Imperial" half way to its true meaning. The outside of the hotel is too great. I just love it too much. Especially the swimming pool is so artistic. But the room is very small, use a lot of dart color which makes the room so dummy. They try to make the room like "Imperial" but it does the opposite. The bathroom and toilet was design illogically. The fan is not working properly. The bed is very high which is very dangerous for your children, also the single bad is really small. I dont like anything inside the room. They do offer you 2 banana, 1 apple and 1 orange for free. I think this is very bad for a claimed to be 5 stars hotel. Also this hotel is very far from the centre, we wasted almost $60 for taxi. Dont trust any taxi driver in the hotel. I was cheated by them getting me to the restaurant where you will be charged a lot more than usual. The hotel food and drink is very expensive too.

reviews.title: Outdated
reviews.text: The hotel is outdated. The phone in the room was dirty and the cord fell off while dialing. There was no shower curtain so the water gets everywhere on the floor. Because the phone had a bad cord, the phone connection was poor when calling room service and the girl on the line yelled at me for complaining I had to repeat myself 5 times. There was no menu in the room for room service- you have to call to request it. It's a Victorian styled hotel but just because it's an old themed hotel doesn't mean things should be dirty and outdated. I would not stay here again. I do have to say they have a good breakfast buffet.
reviews.response:
Dear Ms. Anna,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback following your stay at The IMPERIAL Hotel Vung Tau. While it is appreciated to know that you enjoyed the breakast buffet, sincere regret is extended that the rest of your experience did not meet expectations.

Please accept our heartfelt apology regarding the condition of the in-room telephone, the bathroom setup, and most importantly, the manner of service you experienced when contacting room service. Cleanliness, maintenance, and courteous communication are essential standards, and the concerns you described have been taken very seriously by the hotel’s leadership team for immediate review and follow-up.

Your feedback is deeply valued as it highlights areas that require prompt attention and improvement. Although this stay left you disappointed, it is sincerely hoped that The IMPERIAL may one day have another opportunity to restore your confidence with the level of comfort and service that should always be delivered

reviews.title: Crowded Hotel, No Longer a Place to Chill
reviews.text:
The hotel was overcrowded, mostly with local travelers, even on a Sunday–Monday stay. The pool and restaurant were packed, and for the first time in my life, I had to queue for a buffet breakfast—Monday morning was pure chaos and very uncomfortable. The “Royal” concept seems more like an excuse for outdated facilities. We were upgraded to a Residence Suite, but one of the two smart TVs was so old it barely connected to the internet. Staff were not particularly friendly and often didn’t greet guests when entering or leaving.

We stayed here five years ago during Covid and enjoyed the private beach and seaside restaurant. Now the beach is public, the restaurant has moved indoors, and the charm is gone. With these changes, and the current service, we see no reason to return—perhaps the hotel already has enough guests without us.
reviews.response:
Dear Ms. Quynh,

Thank you for choosing The IMPERIAL Hotel Vung Tau and for sharing your direct and detailed feedback.
It is deeply regretted that the overcrowded atmosphere, the disorder at breakfast, the condition of certain in-room facilities, and the lack of warmth and courtesy from the team left you so disappointed, especially after a previous stay had created a more positive impression.
Your comments have been received with the utmost seriousness and are being carefully reviewed by the relevant departments, with the special attention of the hotel’s leadership, for immediate reflection and improvement.
It is sincerely hoped that a future opportunity may be given to welcome you back and present a stay more in keeping with the standard The IMPERIAL strives to uphold.

Warm regards,
The IMPERIAL Vung Tau Management Team

reviews.title: Not as expected
reviews.text: The hotel is rated 5 stars but it failed to meet our expectation. Staff seem rather unprofessional. They didn't tell us the opening time for breakfast like other hotels. When other staff in the hotel saw us, they rarely smiled or said goodbye when we left. We didnt feel warmly welcomed. Although the hotel is rated 5 stars, except for private beach which is really clean, the facilities are quite old and some are broken if not noticed carefully. For example, baby cot has some parts broken and we didnt get informed when getting it. Also, We had to remind the staff of the baby cot although we did request in advance. When we checked in, they didnt mention anything about it. The faucet wasnt spotlessly clean and the wardrobe was smelly. We wont come back for sure.
reviews.response:
Dear Mrs. Huyen,
Thank you for your time and your constructive feedback. We are disappointed to hear you experienced a shortfall in service during your stay and your comments have been shared with the relevant department leaders for corrective action. We have a regular programme to maintain the rooms and I am pleased to inform that the hotel has plans for more detailed refurbishment later in the year. We take pride in our product and service and hope you choose to stay with us once again.
 Thanks and best regards,
 BeBe Yen Nguyen (Ms.)
Assistant Front Office Manager.

reviews.title: Roaches
reviews.text: Stayed here for 1 night in January 2020. The hotel looked clean and was centrally located to multiple vendors. First walk through of the room seem cleaned until we saw a roach in 2 of the 3 rooms booked. It was crawling across the desk. The bed was comfortable but we were paranoid of bugs after seeing roaches. The shower has a clear window looking into the bedroom but you can get privacy by pulling down the blind. The bathroom is small and claustrophobic to me (I’m 5’3 and 115lbs). There was a slight odor in the hallway to our rooms which is probably because it’s carpeted. The staff were friendly and helpful. The facilities were mediocre.

reviews.title: Bad handling of payment and little sense of hospitality
reviews.text: We booked the hotel via Agoda for 5 nights, accepting a high price because two of the nights are weekend price. The hotel had pre-charged my credits 2 nights, but upon our check in, the staffs insisted that they only charged my card 1 night and want us to pay the rest of the 4 night right away. Only after I complained and with Agoda intervention, they corrected this mistake without an apology. Also, as loyal guests (4 long stays previously), the staff still set us up with one of their worst rooms (right above the club on the 6 floor with hardly any view from the sea). When we refused the room, they  took forever to give us a better room. During the stay, the staffs do not want to accommodate simple request for extra water glass or wine opener. Disappointing!

reviews.title: It is not a 5 star hotel.  Unhappy and waste of money.
reviews.text:
1) When I booked there, I understood that the booking included breakfast. After payment,  I received an email from the hotel that because of Agoda promotion, breakfast was not included and asked me to pay additional cost for breakfast. However, there was not any notice that the price excluded breakfast through the  whole process of my booking.  This is not a manner of 5 star hotel. They should have noticed me at the time of the booking process. 
2) I booked a limousine bus for going back to Saigon. The hotel staff told me that the departure time was 2pm and I should come to the hotel lobby 20 minutes earlier before departure. I  came to the lobby 1:35 pm but the bus already left. I missed the bus and complained on it.  The hotel staff  said that the bus came earlier than departure time.  No sense of  schedule and keeping on time !  I had to waste one hour more for the next bus but the next bus was full of passengers. I had to sit next the driver and went back to Saigon. Very  bad.
reviews.response:
Dear Mr. Kyungsik,
Thank you for taking the time to share your valuable feedback. After receiving your feedback, we did investigation and noticed that you booked through Agoda website. On this point, we are positive that what you made via the website about room type and room benefit will be sent directly to our system without being changed. 

 Regarding to the transportation service,it is not a Limousine  of our hotel.  When assisting you to book the ticket, our Front Desk Agent shared to you that bus service is not from out hotel. We assist guests to call to book ticket only.
We  trained our employee again to ensure that they will convey a clear massage to guests when providing assistance after the incident. We understand how frustrated you were,we tried our best to assist but unfortunately we were not able to fulfill your experience.

We apologize for all inconvenience and hope that you would give us a chance to welcome you back.
Sincerely
Hotel Management

reviews.title: Still have a head ache
reviews.text:
First impression the hotel looked grand. I still remember my room number 304. You open the window and you can see every one walking across the road over pass. This over pass faces the window.
I asked the staff about local places to see and eat, they had little knowledge. The night came and the music down stairs started until after 10:30pm. I could feel the band in my room. Made lots of complaints. No statisfaction. All i can say is if you book this hotel make sure yoiur away from the bar and away from the overpass.
Night market was boring behind the hotel, however there was some good restaurants near the night market.
Pool was great.
Pool restaurant was very slow. 
Haysam Mohtadi
reviews.response:
Dear Haysam,

Thank you very much for taking the time to share your comment following your recent visit at The Imperial Hotel Vung Tau.

On behalf of Managemnet, I have written to you separately and I hope my email fully expresses our sincere apologies and desire to have the opportunity of welcoming you back to The Imperial Hotel in future.

With all good wishes,
On behalf of management.

reviews.title: Good but not really good compared to original price
reviews.text:
We are a group of 10 adults and 2 children so we booked 6 rooms including 2 twin-rooms. 

Pros: The room is large and as beautiful as in the advertising, provided free soft drinks and fruits.
Cons:
- They made us wait from 2pm until 2:45 to get 3 rooms and the last one has to wait until 3:15, only 1 twin-room provided instead 2.
- Among the rooms, 1 room had problem with bathtub (leaked), 1 had unstable hot-cold water in the bath, 1 room had problem with hair-dryer (not working).
- Not really soundproof
reviews.response:
Dear Mr/Ms Hong,

Thank you for staying with us and for sharing your review.

We are pleased to hear that you have enjoyed your stay with us but understand that you experienced some services which did not meet your expectations on this occasion.

Please accept our most sincere apologies. We have taken note of your valuable feedback, which will certainly help us to improve our services.

Once again, thank you for your feedback and we hope to have the pleasure of welcoming you back again soon.

Best Regards,
The Management team
The Imperial Hotel & Residences

reviews.title: Not good value for the money
reviews.text:
We found the room - even though it was evidently an upgrade - to be barren and unwelcoming. The shelves in the living room and cupboards in the kitchen were literally bare. The WiFi router was simply on the floor in a corner. The TV in the bedroom does not align with the bed or any sitting area. The bathroom shower is in a tub that was no water control so the water runs off on to the floor. And breakfast was okay but the service staff was run off their feet due to the low staffing levels so tables remained dirty for a while which created seating issues as people tried to find a table. Overall, for the amount we paid for one night, it felt like we should have literally paid half the rate. 

The pool and beach area were nice and if you stay at a non-beachside hotel, I’d recommend paying the access for the pool and beach area. 

The Front Office Manager, while friendly, explained the Vung Tau market which seemed like excuses for the high cost. 

Save your money; book elsewhere.
reviews.response:
Dear Valued Guest,

Thank you so much for sharing with us about your recent visit to our The IMPERIAL Hotel & Residences.
Please kindly accept our sincere apology for any inconvenience that may caused to you for the hotel facilities such as kitchen amenities, wifi router, TV in the bedroom, bathroom shower and also our service. As we strive to provide the highest level of service everyday; we truly regret to learn that we did not meet your expectation for the delivered facilities service. Our team appreciates your candid feedback that we will be sure to address and we are constantly working to make improvements on the quality wherever possible.

Many thanks again, we look forward to welcoming you back very soon when your travels bring you to this beautiful city.

Sincerely,
The Managerment Team.

reviews.title: $100+ a night for less than 3 star hotel service
reviews.text:
I booked a room for more than $100 thinking that I need the room to use their private beach. I wish I did not use that option.
There private beach is clean with a nice large size swimming pool. The hotel charge $12.50 per adult and $7.5 for children. Guests can stay all day.  You don't need to be a beach guests to use this service.
The room itself was small with old furniture, smelly carpet, very small twin beds. The carpet on the hallway was dirty and smelly. The hotel is not a five star hotel definitely.
Front desk staff look nice but not efficient when it comes to customer service.
We ordered food at the downstairs restaurant. It was expensive for what it has to offer.
I do not recommend or stay here again.
reviews.response:
Dear Dan,
Thank you for staying with us for recent visiting.
Your comment is highly appreciated. We took note your comment to improve better service.
For your kind information, currently the cleaning of the carpet is on going. All our staffs will be trained to provide better customer service.

Hope to serve you better next time and we look forward to seeing you in the near future again.
Best regards,
The IMPERIAL Hotel Management Team

reviews.title: Nice seaview room
reviews.text: Hotel looks nicer in pictures than in person.  Facilities are bit run down, actually.  Seaview room is nice, off you just look straight out to the ocean, just don't look down to the other rooftops. Breakfast buffet is bleh, not big selection. Gym has a boxing rink, which is kind of cool. There is a breach front pool, where you have to cross the bridge over the street, with cabana houses around the pool for a fee to rent. Then there is the hotel pool, which I liked better, in the middle surrounded by hotel rooms. The hotel rooms hallway kind of look haunted.  Spacious room and bathroom.  Although our sink leaked and the room phone didnt work, but were fixrd within the hour.  Do I care to stay again, probably not.

reviews.title: Maximum four stars
reviews.text:
I don't know who give the stars in Vietnam, but when you compare this hotel to 5 stars hotels in Vietnam, it is obvious that it is not the same level.
Rooms are old fashion and already damaged. Difficulties to get hot water.
Breakfast service quite poor for a 5 stars. In addition very crowded on Saturdays and Sundays.

reviews.text: very good

reviews.text:

reviews.text: It was very pleasant stay at the hotel.  If I avoided rainy season, it would have been gorgeous stay.  3000 vnd for one day mortar bike rental was not cheapest among others, however they let me extend many hours.  It was very good.  Overall, very satisfied with stay.
reviews.positives: private beach, good service, nice pool

reviews.title: convenient hotels with large bed rooms
reviews.text: This hotel is very convenient to travel arround Vung Tau. However, the breakfast buffet is not as well as my expectation

reviews.title: Great stay even though check in was very slow
Good but Not excellence
reviews.text:
I think that Imperial is the best in Vung Tau but it doenst mean it is really great.
The room is cozy and full of classic things but quite small.
The shower door couldn't closed well so water overflowed to outside. My husbund broke a cup and they asked us to pay about US$90. I think it's too much. Everything is not for customer but just their decoration.
Also I was very disappointed about the breakfast. I dont have nothing to eat except bread and butter. Comparing the price, everythig is not enough to my expectation except beach.

reviews.title: bad services!!!!!!!
reviews.text:
We stayed here for one night,and used two restaurants for dinner and lunch.
There are cute rooms every floors,but very uncomfortable services every where in a hotel when we visit. Service is less than 3stars hotels......!!!!!

reviews.title: The staff don't know how to smile
reviews.text: Visited the hotel at the festival, and the hotel is the host. However, the busy air made them hardly smile with guest. When we checked in, the manager kept telling another guest about the bus, any other receptionist was busy typing and no one turned up her head to see us. They didn't even bother say sorry for making us waiting. After getting into our room, we had a snap because a long journey. Suddenly some of the staff knocked the door and said that they wanted to add some more items into our mini bar (though we hadn't request). And at last, the check-out took us about 30 minutes. The hotel is best at anything, but very bad at staff performance.
reviews.negatives: Staff hardly smiled, check-out took very long time, staff bothered guest when they want to add items in the in-room mini bar

reviews.title: Trang
reviews.text: Imperial needs to compare with other 5 star hotels for standard
reviews.positives: Pool is nice for the kids even though we have to walk over the bridge across the main road

reviews.title: Outdated 4 star hotel at a 5 star price
reviews.text: Stay was ok. Staff was professional in booking a return trip to Ho Chi Ming on the hydrofoil. Although, asking them for restaurant recommendations was frustrating to say the least. Booking a suite with a sea view was a joke. Biggest complaints were a lack of extra towels and water in room and the strong smell of sewage coming from the bathroom.  Very unsatisfactory from a five star property.

reviews.title: No security
reviews.text: The second day we were their in the hotel entrance my friends phone was snatched out of her hand , no on seemed to care it was right in front of the security

reviews.title: dsiappoited with 5 stars hotel
reviews.text: I am so disappointed with 5 stars hotel. There are so many local people...so noisy and no education. Room is so small and old time decoration. Never come back again .

reviews.title: Do not wish to come back
reviews.positives: Nice interior with paintings on the walls, carpets on the floors and statues in the lobby and around on e of the 2 outdoors pools- imperial style

reviews.title: Lovely 5-star hotel but with snooty service
reviews.text: We have 2 young kids and a toddler and they charged us for an extra child, very unaccommodating!
reviews.positives: 2 great swimming pools, one by the sea

reviews.title: Old fashion Hotel but good maintained
reviews.text: After some problems with the cleanness and a relocation in an upgraded room the impression was ok.

reviews.title: May be one of great hotel in Vung Tau
reviews.text: My husband & I were disappointed; we didn’t have a pleasant stay. The staffs were not so friendly and their services were not up to 5 star standard. We did really surprise by the so call sea view room, what we can see, when we overlooking at the balcony, was that a busy main road and the roof of the building below, even if they change the room from 501 to 701 for us after complained, it didn’t make much difference.  The décor of the room is not our type. It just over does everything; it only puts all the reproduction of European art together; it lacks of nature beauty. Their staff bills us without checking if they have filled any stock of the mini bar in our room, they only checked and cancelled the bill after receiving complained. Their breakfast buffet was good but not the dinner buffet. The dinner buffet is not value for money. It lacks of variety of food; food quality was not high.
reviews.positives: Only take one & half hour from HCMC to Vung Tau by hydrofoil ferry

reviews.title: a bloated pretense of something old english
reviews.text: we just don't get this place at all.  why try to replicate some stuffy european castle with a bunch of cheap reproductions of renaissance art?  why not have an electrical plug in the bathroom for my shaver?  why have a big picture window in the bathroom that looks into the bedroom. so that we can watch each other on the toilet?   why not have a ventilator in the bathroom that works?
reviews.positives: bedding didn't stink of perfumed detergent, great view of the buildings next door
reviews.negatives: smokers inside restaurant, screaming kids everywhere, staff unfriendly

reviews.title: Poor experience - Not worth the cost.
reviews.text:
1. My family arrived at the hotel by shuttle, and the hotel staff refused to let the car stop at the main lobby, insisting that we park outside even though it was raining quite heavily and my family has a 5-year-old girl. Our luggage was left outside in the rain and got quite wet. The staff only provided one umbrella for the three of us and explained that it was not possible to park in the lobby due to the busy check-in time??? This is a very unreasonable and unjustifiable reason for a 5-star hotel.
2. The welcome drink service is very unprofessional here. Guests have to go to the bar to order a welcome drink??? And the bar does not always have available seats to sit, so is it really a welcome drink??
3. Due to the rain, we had to move by a 16-seat vehicle from the main lobby to the Imperial residence, and the 16-seater had a door malfunction, unable to open??? Guests had to wait more than 20 minutes to get on the vehicle to check in.
This is truly a poor experience for the service of a 5-star hotel.
reviews.response:
Kính chào Anh Hồ,
Chúng tôi rất tiếc khi biết rằng trải nghiệm lưu trú tại khách sạn không đạt được sự mong đợi của Anh. Chúng tôi chân thành xin lỗi về những bất tiện mà Anh đã gặp phải. Với mục tiêu đưa khách sạn The Imperial Vũng Tàu trở thành điểm đến hàng đầu về dịch vụ chất lượng và nghỉ dưỡng sang trọng, chúng tôi luôn coi trọng ý kiến đóng góp và cam kết sẽ nỗ lực cải thiện chất lượng dịch vụ để mang đến trải nghiệm tốt hơn trong tương lai. 
Một lần nữa, chúng tôi xin lỗi về bất kỳ sự bất tiện nào và cảm ơn sự thông cảm của Anh. Chúng tôi hy vọng sẽ được chào đón Anh cùng gia đình trở lại trong thời gian sớm nhất với một trải nghiệm tốt hơn và đáng nhớ hơn.
Trân trọng,
Thay mặt ban quản lý khách sạn 
Alex Linh Dang (Mr)
Assistant Front Office Manager

reviews.title: Bad and rude staff
reviews.text: We can’t believe this is 5 star hotel. The hotel facility is old. The wifi is weak in the room. The staff is rude and have bad attitude. It took them 10 minutes to find out my room number when I left the key.  The only good thing is the private beach with free umbrella.
reviews.response:

With the goal of making The Imperial Vung Tau Hotel a leading destination for quality service and luxury relaxation, we greatly value your feedback and are committed to making continuous improvements to enhance the service quality for a better experience in the future. We hope you will give us the opportunity to serve you better on your future stays.
Sincerely,
On behalf of the Hotel Management Team
Alex Linh Dang (Mr)
Assistant Front Office Manager

reviews.title: Noisy
reviews.text: On one side of the Hotel, there is loud major heavy equipment construction happening that starts at 3:00 am.

reviews.title: Unfair Treatment
reviews.text:
Paid extra for the room with transportation services from Saigon to Vung Tau but was denied services both to and fro. To, no one reply my message of booming from the chat. Fro, reception told me no available transport. If this is the case, why collect extra money from me.

Sound proof issue. You can hear door banging, passerby Loud voices,and the kids noise at the pool.

Bell boy is wearing a traditional Vietnamese uniform. They are very attentive.

Breakfast is not so crowded at 9am. They serve a decent variety of food.

Housekeeping staff was super polite, however they forgot to replenish the minibar and rubbish on the desk was not clear.

We use the overhead bridge to the beach. The beach is very clean, but it is abit crowded. There is not enough serving staff by the beach, need to go in the restaurant to make the order of food and drink.

Room aircon was cold. But since it was blowing directly facing the bed, we need to turn up the degree causing it to be hot, fan speed can't be adjust.

Do bring your own shampoo and shower gel as the one that provide isn't 5star graded.

Safety box was not secured onto the cabinet, as it is small and very light, anyone can just lift it and go. Unable to use for valuable item.

The decoration of the hotel is nice. Leather sofa in the lobby was torn, need to repair abit. Room decor was very classic. Similar to France classic design.

The staff at the reception lacks of smile. It will be good if they could serve with a smile.

Overall a decent hotel, but wouldn't grade it as 5star.
reviews.response:
Dear Kenneth,
Thank you for choosing The Imperial Hotel as your accommodation. Our hotel values put guest satisfaction as top priority and we are committed to our guests. We of course would like to do the same for you.  We sincerely apology about the services during your stay which could not meet your satisfaction. And your feedback is precious point for head of management know how to improve and make exceptional services to customers. Once again, thank you for choosing us and we look forward to serving you again. 
Thanks and Best regards! 
Maya Thao (Ms.) 
Duty Manager.

reviews.title: Definitely not 5 star
reviews.text: The place may have been 5 stars at one time but the place is getting to be a bit run down. First the room kitchen area cabinets were  broken and had electrical wires hanging. Second the bathtub didn’t hold water and there was no shower curtain. It did seem to be designed to not have a curtain but it didn’t work all that well. Third it was next to impossible to get drinks at the pool and finally got the lifeguard to do it for us. He was very nice and helpful. Finally my fourth and last gripe was the quality of the buffet breakfast. The food was cold and of not good quality. My sourdough bread was still doughy as in not baked completely.
reviews.response:
Dear Ms. Hanh, 
Thank you for your time and your constructive feedback. We are disappointed to hear you experienced a shortfall in service during your stay and your comments have been shared with the relevant department leaders for corrective action. Your comments give us the opportunity to improve our service and I hope we have the chance to serve your better at The Imperial Hotel Vung Tau during the next trip. 
We are looking forward to welcoming you back in the Vung Tau. 
Thanks and best regards, 
BeBe Yen Nguyen (Ms.) 
Assistant Front Office Manager.

reviews.title: Good

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: I booked this hotel for my parents and their friends. As i was not travelling with them so i agreed to let them collect cash payment and void my card payment. They told me the refund would be in 7-10 days but i still haven’t received any. I called them and was asked to wait for few more days. That’s terrible.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience, after receiving the feedback, we have asked the bank for a quick refund, please check again and let's us know if have any mistakes. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome your family again at The Imperial Hotel & Resort Vung Tau.
Thanks and best regards 
BeBe Yen Nguyen (Ms.)
Assistant Front Office Manager.

reviews.title: Old hotel
reviews.text: The facilities quality went down vs 5 starts rating and it' not worthy for the price

reviews.title: Nice staff

The place is a little old but not that bad. The outside bar is lowkey kind of a ripoff. I recommend booking last minute since they significantly drop the rates. Booked for like 200 USD 2 months in advance, rooms were like 100 USD one week before. And the hotel is massive so I doubt its ever fully booked unless it's High season for Vietnamese locals.

Even though they let us check in one room first put seem after long drive everybody tired and have to wait for the room so long . And we only got room at 14:45pm. reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
They serve cooked western breakfast, but everything except the egg (cooked to order) was stone cold,  no heating lamps no hot plates etc.  They had a plate heater but it was not even plugged in.    Even the coffee americano was cold, they keep it in a metal open top jug then top up the thermos type jug from there after it has gone cold!     

Raised this with restaurant managers 3 days running and they could not have shown less interest and made absolutely no changes.  Hopeless.   It's a shame as some staff are trying to do a good job with managers who clearly do not care.

reviews.title: Disappointing stay
reviews.text:
Stayed 2 nights 16-18 Aug at Imperial Hotel Vung Tau (Residence Tower)
Struck with food poisoning after buffet breakfast and then spent 1/2 day in hospital.
Shifu Chinese Restaurant was understaffed and service was average.
Imperial Plaza only had 1 floor of retail stores when it was advertised as having 4 floors whilst making booking, and on the in-house guest service directory and also on their website.
Check in time was listed as 2pm although on arrival we were told it was 3pm and had to wait, the lifts usually always required a long wait. I felt hotel staff in general were certainly not up to 5 star service quality. Facilities like the pools, gym and imperial spa were nice, however, my wife and I were disappointed on several counts, will not be returning and would not recommend.

reviews.title: Huge disappoinment
reviews.text:
My stay with Premium Deluxe Ocean view, with one smell toilet as a starting point.
The hotel has a Pool party, and they tell us not to use the Beach pool by 5:30 pm, in fact, it was total chao at the pool as they are setting up the music stage.  The party is sold at 550K VND per ticket for all people. We dont use it but how can the hotel cut off our time and service at the pool, we paid full money via agoda and the hotel did not supply the full service, what kind of service is that?
Then, they play the music till 11:30 PM which we cannot sleep ( our room is at front ocean view) and I have to call reception, they told me the party is over at 12pm and they feel sorry for that, as this party is hosted by the management. Come on, this time they makes me feel Imperial doesnt provide the required services, and they dont care about their guest.  I paid full money to use service here, but they dont supply all. I have been guest more than 10 years here, this is totally upset, and I will never coming back, pls consider carefully or any date before u book the hotel!
reviews.response:
Dear Valued Guest,

Thank you for your honest feedback; we appreciate all critiques as it greatly assists us in our commitment to constant improvement.

Regarding your comments, we understand your frustration with the beach party during your most recent stay. The hotel considers this time period to be the busiest of the year, however we still strive to ensure all hotel entertainments are met out returned guest's expectation. 

We acknowledge that we could do better in managing the situation within 3 or 4 days in advance. There should have been more clear communication by our reservation team and guest, 
Moving forward, we will also be organizing these types of entertainment during our peak periods to ensure that all of our hotel activies are met guest's expectations.

We sincerely apologize for the shortcomings and welcoming you back in the near future.

Best Regards,
The Management Team

reviews.title: Cosy, comfy and easy
reviews.text:
The hotel theme is of a Victorian style which offers a serene yet majestic feel. Yet it is because of this theme, the room corridor portray an eerie and spooky feel whenever I walk along it. Oil paintings and portraits couple with spot lighting magnify the hair rising effect.

The room is comfy and cosy, well carpeted, again in Victorian style. Flatscreen is a tad too small to view from bed though. Nonetheless, basic amenities are available. The complimentary grenade like bottled water is a little too small and inconvenient, conventional would be more welcome.

Breakfast offers a reasonable spread, nothing fancy with the usual basics but I would love to see them improve on this. Chinese restaurant is fine by it own standard, headed by a Guangzhou chef, real authentic Chinese Cuisine can be request if one required. 

There is a small mall beside the hotel with a supermarket where one can buy basic groceries like snack and titbits or toiletries which find it very handy. Definitely one of the better hotel around the region and worth returning to again.
reviews.positives: Nice rooms, unique decor, and quiet.

The pool and grounds are well kept and comfortable.  There is a kids centre that is only basic but can keep children entertained for a short period.

reviews.title: Staff was great, location great, rooms were well-used.
reviews.text:
Staff greeted us and were friendly and helpful. 

The location is great - across from the beach in Vung Tàu. 

The rooms are small, and there were several damaged items in ours - the towel rack in the bathroom was bent, the bed frame was chipped, the safe would stick while both closing and opening. ..oh, and the ceiling fans are, "just for decoration" - they do not work. The sink and tub appeard to be well-used, with scratches and excessive wear at the drains of both. 

The bedding felt moist and one of the towels had reddish spots in the fabric. 

Lastly, the air conditioning in the lobby was not set to be comfortable and the elevators had no air conditioning at all. 

As stated, the staff was friendly and tried to be helpful and the location is beautiful but it lacks attention to detail that I would expect at a 5 star facility.

reviews.title: The hotel is unfortunatly no longer worth 5 stars
reviews.text:
I have been here three times. Unfortunately I can't no longer recommend the hotel. It is impossible to relax at the beach because every other day there is an event where for the entire afternoon someone screams very loud into a microphone in vietnamese. I have complained to staff about it after the first day but the day after that it was exactly the same.
This is very disrespectful of the people how only want to relax and not acceptable for a 5 star hotel. Additional the WIFI is very slow. Very sad but this is was my last time staying here.
reviews.response:
Dear Phillip,
With regret we have taken a note of your experience at The Imperial Hotel Vung Tau.Please accept our sincere apologies for the inconveniences experienced in regards of noise from the event and slow Wifi. Its speed is very easily affected by surround nature so it is not stable sometimes even though our hotel have the best Wifi supplier and package in the town. Knowing about this issue, our hotel is always ready with speedy portable 4G router to support guests. We wished that you had shared with us about this issue so that we could provide to you. In regard of noise, we sincerely apologize about it when event came after event. We talked to the organizer in concern of noise and the organizer reduce the volume however unfortunately it could be heard from distance. 
Once again please accept our apologies. Thank you again for sharing your experience, and we sincerely hope to have the opportunity to better serve you in the near future.
Warm regards,
The Hotel Management

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
In general the hotel is clean, with nice swimming pool.  Food is just OK, breakfast buffet not so much choices compare to Ho Tram Strips. or any other hotels around Vung Tau like Intourco, Cao or Pullman.

One thing I noticed and it upset me the most was hotel guests got divided into two groups, Vietnamese guests won't get paper  napkin and white cloth napkin (instead they got dark red cloth napkins, which I think they didn't have to clean those carefully).  But foreigner guests got their table set up with a stack of paper napkins and white cloth napkins.  Their tables got more attention and got dirty plates removed faster and more often.   Another group of Vietnameses, but they spoke Chinese then, they got their tables set up same as foreigner guests, and got additional care, etc....  I don't think this is a smart way of doing business.  Not deserving with 4,5 stars rating,  very unprofessional, I wouldn't recommend this to anyone of my family or friends.

Sincerely,
The Management Team

reviews.title: Nice hotel close to beach
reviews.text: We were 3 mates staying at the hotel for 1 night only. The hotel is across the road from the beach but has it's own beach club set up with pool and restaurant on the actual beach. They really broke the mould when they built this hotel. The "Versace like" theme throughout the entire hotel is over the top and tacky. We thought it was hilarious and that was part of the fun. Check in was polite but extremely slow. Room - fabulous. Big and comfortable. Decent bathroom with good shower. Common areas - clean. Breakfast in restaurant - fabulous. Staff - friendly and competent. Beachside pool - wonderful. The price we thought was a bit steep for Vung Tau. We enjoyed our short stay and would return.

reviews.title: Boutique Hotel in a Beach
reviews.text:
Overall a good experience!  Hotel service is fast.  The imperial plaza is nearby and so is a grocery store.  
Private beach gives feeling of security while the pools offer alternatives once you are done with salty water.
The price is a bit on the high side though considering some facilities have started to deteriorate and the repair work can be improved.
Breakfast selection, while varied, is more catered to locals.
reviews.positives: Unique Decors everywhere - feels like 18th Century

reviews.title: Descent hotel with beautiful beach
reviews.text: i've stayed in famous colonial hotels in saigon and hanoi, but this hotel has best antique design and romantic mood - i'm pretty sure that it can satisfy women's taste very well. imperial has beautiful, well managed, clean private beach with pool on beach side but you should cross big road by pedestrian overpass. i love bath tub and shower booth in the room but closet is relatively small and their laundry service is not satisfactory for 5 star hotel.
reviews.positives: Antique design, beautiful private beach and large shopping mall

reviews.title: Decent Stay with Great Location
reviews.text:
The location is really really good, you’re literally a stone’s throw from the beach. While the staff were friendly and quite helpful, cleanliness could have been better. They were, however, undergoing some renovation so possibly this was not representative of their usual standards. 
It’s a mixed bag for value though, I’m afraid, some aspects may not justify the price, especially since there is plenty of stiff competition along the same beach road.

Thank you very much for your feedback and for rating us "Very Good". We truly appreciate your time and comments.

We would like to clarify that The IMPERIAL Vung Tau is proud to be one of the few hotels in the city with direct access to a private beach. The beach is exclusively reserved for our in-house guests and can be reached conveniently via the dedicated over-bridge connecting the hotel to the beachfront. Our staff are always present at both ends of the bridge to guide and assist guests safely down to the beach area.

We sincerely apologize for any misunderstanding and hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back soon, so you can fully enjoy all the exclusive facilities we offer.

Sincerely Yours

Magdalene Lim
Director Of Operations
The IMPERIAL Hotel Vung Tau

reviews.text: Good. More variety of food would be better. Especially at buffet breakfast, mainly Asian and pork mixed with everything, which we don't eat.

reviews.title: Ostentatiously fake decor but decent rooms
reviews.text: The fake Renaissance/Victorian decor is ridiculously out of place, tacky and garish. But once you get past the decor, there is much to enjoy about the hotel. The rooms are huge and well stocked with all the things needed for a comfortable stay. The lap pool is tucked away in a quiet part of the hotel and conducive for a peaceful swim. Swimming is probably best done at the hotel pool. While the hotel is conveniently connected to the beach club, it's more crowded there and less tranquil than the brochure pictures portray. The beach also isn't that fantastic compared to other beaches in Southeast Asia.
reviews.positives: Nice lap pool, excellent rooms - big, comfortable and well-equipped

reviews.title: Short Getaway
reviews.text: We stayed 2 nights at The Imperial Hotel Vung Tau and it was quite nice. The Hotel has it unique decorations within the lobby area and as well as the room. The highlight of the Hotel are the beach side of the pool with private beach area which is impressive for Vung Tau back beach areas. Breakfast at hotel are very good with many varieties to choose from. Staff are friendly and helpful, just the hotel itself showed it aged and in needs of renovation.
reviews.positives: 1. Private beach across the road from hotel. 2.Ttwo side of pools, beach side and within the hotel side. 3. Room were quite unique decorated.

reviews.title: My Stay at Imperial Vung Tau was normal only
reviews.text: The overall stay was average. The breakfast food quality was disappointing and given the fact that I still had to fork out to pay the breakfast for my relatives, I think it will be better to eat out at a restaurant although you need to take a taxi. The hotel room is quite worn out and small - I booked a room with 2 beds for 3 person. The double bed is very small and cramped - worth than those 3 stars hotels' bed size for a king size bed. I had to give up my bed for my baby as it is so small.  The lift is very old and dark and eerie. The ocean view was great though. I will only rate it as a four star hotel at most.

There is no water sprayer for toilet used, and lavabo get yellowing around the edge so feeling a bit dirty.

reviews.title: Nice, relaxing weekend break
reviews.text: I go on business trips to Vung Tau and decided to stay the weekend in the Imperial as a relaxing trip away from Saigon. The hotel is lovely, very regal. Rooms and beds very comfortable and clean. Had a nice massage and SPA was lovely. Highlight of the hotel is the pool area and beach which is great. Ate at the poolside bar which was reasonable prices and pretty decent but not a huge choice. I also asked for 3 different beers of which they had none!. Only disappointment was breakfast- very limited choices and quality for a hotel of this stature. All in highly recommended weekend break.

reviews.title: Some points of dissatisfaction.
reviews.text:
My family only stays at Imperial when we go to Vung Tau. It’s enough to show our love and trust in this hotel. There’s little to criticize. However, this time it was a bit chaotic. I booked a 46m2 room through Agoda for two nights at nearly ***, with breakfast (I always book rooms through Agoda and have never encountered any issues). Yet upon check-in, they said they couldn’t find the Agoda booking, even though the credit card had already been charged, and the booking status showed it was paid. Ultimately, the receptionist informed us that we were being switched to a 102m2 room, but without breakfast. Honestly, for three people, we didn’t need such a large room, but I was tired and wanted to go up to the room to rest, so I didn’t want to clarify it further. However, it felt a bit odd, like they ran out of 46m2 rooms and we had to settle for the 102m2 room without breakfast. In the end, for the next two breakfasts, we had to scramble to catch a taxi to eat outside, because when calculating, each breakfast buffet would cost us over 300k, which seemed quite unreasonable, especially when the room rate was nearly 8.5 million for one night. 
- Secondly, the hotel’s wifi is extremely weak. With such a large hotel using the same wifi, it’s almost unusable.
- Thirdly, the bed linens had an unpleasant smell. Last time I came to the hotel five months ago, there was also that unpleasant fishy smell in the bedding. I didn’t mention it out of hesitation but it was the same this time. For a 5-star hotel at this price, the bedding should smell luxurious and fragrant, not like this 😅
Aside from these issues, there’s also the wind. The pool area is always extremely cold due to the wind. The structure of the buildings at Imperial channels the wind directly into the pool area. In the middle of summer, the cold wind can be shocking and chilling. Anyone sensitive to the cold could easily get sick. 
Nevertheless, Imperial remains our choice whenever my family goes to relax in Vung Tau, with a stunning sea view, especially from the 15th floor and up in the Residence area. The staff from the front desk to the service are all perfect. I hope that next time the concerns mentioned above can be cleared up, especially regarding the cold wind, so we can chill by the pool and work off our meals before the next one 🥰
reviews.response:
Kính chào chị Huyền,

Cảm ơn chị và gia đình đã luôn lựa chọn The IMPERIAL Hotel Vung Tau mỗi khi đến Vũng Tàu, đồng thời dành thời gian chia sẻ phản hồi rất chi tiết sau kỳ lưu trú gần nhất. Sự tin tưởng lâu dài của chị là điều hết sức quý giá, và thật vui khi view biển, đặc biệt từ các tầng cao khu Residence, vẫn luôn để lại ấn tượng đẹp trong trải nghiệm của gia đình.

Tuy nhiên, khách sạn cũng thành thật xin lỗi vì những bất tiện chị đã gặp phải trong lần lưu trú này, đặc biệt là việc không tìm thấy đặt phòng Agoda khi làm thủ tục nhận phòng, sự thay đổi hạng phòng nhưng không bao gồm bữa sáng, chất lượng Wi-Fi chưa ổn định, cùng mùi khó chịu từ chăn ga trong phòng. Những góp ý này đã được ghi nhận với sự quan tâm đặc biệt của Ban Lãnh đạo khách sạn và chuyển đến các bộ phận liên quan để khẩn trương rà soát, làm rõ và cải thiện.

Khách sạn cũng rất tiếc khi điều kiện gió tại khu vực hồ bơi đã ảnh hưởng đến sự thoải mái của gia đình chị. Những chia sẻ thẳng thắn và chi tiết như của

reviews.title: The beachside swimming pool has disappeared.
reviews.text:
When I first came to Vietnam, the leisure of the hotel's beachfront pool and the memories have faded away together.

Thanks to the room upgrade, I was assigned to a larger room, but I gave up using the bathtub as the hot water kept coming and going.

There was no room service guide booklet, so I had some difficulties in ordering room service.

The staff were friendly, and there were many people, but I did not feel it was significantly more crowded than before.

Staying in the residence side makes using the hotel facilities a bit inconvenient, such as accessing breakfast, the gym, and moving to the parking lot...
reviews.response:
Dear Mr.changyoo,

Thank you very much for sharing your detailed feedback regarding your recent stay at The Imperial Hotel Vung Tau.

We truly regret that the absence of our former beachside pool affected your experience and the special memories you once associated with it. We understand how meaningful those moments can be, especially when returning to a destination you have previously enjoyed. Please be assured that changes to our facilities are carefully considered as part of our long term enhancement plans, and we sincerely apologize for any disappointment caused.

While we are pleased that you received a room upgrade, we are very sorry to hear about the inconsistent hot water supply. This is certainly not the standard we aim to deliver, and our Engineering team has been informed to thoroughly inspect and resolve this matter.

We also apologize for the inconvenience caused by the missing in-room dining guide. Your comment is extremely valuable, and we will review our room setup proc

reviews.title: Nice place but in the middle of no where!!!
reviews.text: Stayed 6 nights. Staff is excellent! Facilities are great. Despite some negative comments we read from reviews concerning the beach, we thought the beach was very nice! Staff, hotel, food ( reasonable price and delicious considering it’s a 5 star hotel) great place! Would only recommend for no more than 5 nights for a family as once you’ve used and become familiar with the facilities, there’s little to do as there’s not even a convenience store within miles! Thankfully we rented a scooter which is rare as most local motorbike shops won’t rent motorbikes to strangers. Wonderful getaway for 2-5 nights... no more than that. Breakfast was amazing! Best I’ve had including Sheraton, Hilton, grand Hyatt. By far the best! The food quality is excellent. Room service staff are amazing as well. Due to a slight issue, I needed to deal with the manager who was extremely friendly and courteous! Thanks for everything!

Những bình luận hay nhận xét của Anh sẽ là đóng góp vô cùng quý báu đối cho những nỗ lực cải thiện chất lượng dịch vụ trong xuyên suốt quá trình hoạt động khách sạn. Ban quản lý The IMPERIAL Hotel & Residence.

reviews.title: Great location, good service, very old and not money worth facilities
reviews.text:
Chosen lots of time when staying in Vung tau, but only cause there are no other equal choice in terms of all conditions ( location, service, food choices..). However, the most critical and unacceptable point is the hotel facilities including:
- Bed stuffs: the Louis inspiration bed is ridiculous tall with decor angles will be easily to hurt your knees or legs, and very hard to climb for kids. The mattress is like never be changed since the hotel established ( guess around nearly 20 years now). It will be guaranteed to tortured your back and make you feel like seasick cause the floppy :( . I have mentioned and gave comments lots of time to staffs, management but nothing changed. 
- TV: extremely small ( 25 inches?) and you will need the horoscope to watching it. 
- Room and corridors are also old and you will easily to witness the rusty or fall out bar along the way.
It still attracts the guests until other competitors are existing so the owner can made up their mind now.

reviews.title: It was a wee bit disappointing
reviews.text:
I stayed in Imperial twice in the last 6 years and always loved it. I always make it a point to recommend this hotel to friends. However, it's been 2 years since my last visit. I noticed the hotel is getting a wee bit older and probably required a fair bit of maintenance. I stayed on the 6th floor and the moment the lift door opens, there was an overwhelming musty smell all the way to my room. I noticed a blower on the floor all the time. I guess to get rid of the smell?

Food was so-so at the hotel but it has always been so.

However, I need to make a point to praise the staff there. They are really polite and very prompt in their service.

Rooms were very comfortable though might need abit of 'modern sprucing up' as it personally seemed a little dated as compared to many other similar rated resort hotels. reviews.title: Best in Vung Tau for family with kids
reviews.text:
A bit tired already with odd interior design but service and exceptional location on the beach makes it perfect for your short stay in Vung Tau.  

2 very good swimming pools - one is just on the beach are perfect for kids fun. Weekend can be very crowded - avoid that time if you can.

Service is very good, efficient and with a smile. Breakfast will satisfy your appetite to Vietnamese or Western cuisine.
Pricy for Vietnam but Vung Tau doesnt give you plenty of choices.

reviews.text: It's not a first time for me to stay in this hotel and I decide to stay again because it is really nice hotel with staff performance, nice pool, beach and service . Room are comfortable and decoration like imperial style, quiet and very good for relaxation  Recommended for all people and for sure I will stay again next time.

reviews.title: The only 5 stars Vung Tau
reviews.text:
This has got to be the nicest hotel in Vung Tau. It has a European theme written all over the structure cater to Local from HCMC and foreigner. 

It is not perfect and there are certainly much rooms for improvement. 

But overall it is certainly still worth giving this place a stay. There is hardly much reason not to.
reviews.positives:
- Lovely rooms, very nicely decorated hotel and some good staff as well.

- Lovely swimming pool, one for swimming laps and one to relax... the overall environment relaxing.

- High tech gym and with a boxing ring as well.
reviews.negatives:
- For a 5 star hotel, simple value added service such as free hotel pick up from ferry terminal would be good. Given it is such a short distance. 

- The lobby area, lifts, and corridor are warm as if there was no air-conditoner. 

- Given it is quite a new hotel, strangly there are area of cracks here and there from the hotel paintwork.

- Staff do not understand or make one egg omelette for breakfast and never bother to ask again.

reviews.title: Great hotel for holiday
reviews.text: I had a great time staying here. The only thing I would mention is that no special arrangements were made for my anniversary, even though I contacted the hotel weeks before my arrival. However, I understand that this depends on availability, especially since it was during the national holidays. Apart from that, the room was lovely, the private beach was a big plus, and the pools were amazing.

My family always chooses Imperial to enjoy our annual vacations. This year, I have two reviews: GOOD & NEEDS IMPROVEMENT.
1. Good: the staff's attitude is lovely, friendly, and enthusiastic.
2. Needs improvement: the sanitary equipment and utensils are old, the shower head is difficult to use, the kettle leaks, the wifi is weak, the rooms next to construction areas are noisy, making it hard to sleep well. Shuttle service: the quotation states it includes shuttle service, but it does not; if full, they will not provide shuttle support; the breakfast area is too crowded, often requiring a wait.
reviews.response:
Kính chào chị Ngan,

Chân thành cảm ơn chị và gia đình đã luôn tin tưởng lựa chọn The IMPERIAL Hotel Vung Tau cho những kỳ nghỉ trong năm. Khách sạn rất trân trọng sự ghi nhận của chị dành cho thái độ phục vụ dễ thương, thân thiện và nhiệt tình của đội ngũ nhân viên.

Đồng thời, những góp ý của chị về các trải nghiệm chưa trọn vẹn cũng đã được ghi nhận với sự quan tâm đặc biệt và chuyển đến Ban Quản Lý cùng các bộ phận liên quan để khẩn trương rà soát, làm rõ và cải thiện. Cả vấn đề về tiện nghi trong phòng lẫn một số khâu dịch vụ liên quan đều đang được xem xét nghiêm túc, như một phần trong nỗ lực không ngừng nhằm mang đến trải nghiệm tốt nhất cho mỗi khách hàng.

Hy vọng sẽ sớm có cơ hội được đón tiếp chị và gia đình quay trở lại để cảm nhận một kỳ nghỉ trọn vẹn và thoải mái hơn.

Trân trọng,

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: I booked through Agoda and it asked me to pick between smoking and non-smoking room. I picked smoking room since my father smokes. When i came, the receptionist said none of their rooms allows smoking, but the traveler can smoke at the balcony. Ok, that was acceptable to me but when we asked for the ashtray, someone called to our room and said that "we do not allow smoking at all". Its quite annoy that the communication is not consistent in all channels and from one to other staff. Other than that, everything else was nice. I like the area where we can sit outside by the pool.
reviews.response:
Dear Mr.Duong,
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback.

First and foremost, The IMPERIAL Hotel sincerely apologizes for the confusion and inconvenience regarding the smoking policy during your recent stay. We truly understand how important it is for communication to be clear and consistent.

Kindly allow us to clarify that The IMPERIAL Hotel is a 100% non-smoking hotel, and this policy is applied to all room types across our property. However, on the Agoda booking form, there is a default option allowing guests to choose between "Smoking" and "Non-smoking" rooms. This is a global default format set by Agoda, and although The IMPERIAL Hotel has formally raised this issue with Agoda, we have been informed that they currently do not have plans to customize or remove this option per individual property.

We truly regret that this inconsistency caused you inconvenience and led to mixed messages from our team. Additionally, we sincerely apologize for the lack of sensitivity in

reviews.title: A comfortable stay
reviews.text:
Overall, we had a good stay. There were only two annoyances.

A. The light switch in one bedroom, was turning lights on and off in another bedroom (of a multi room apartment), which was a little frustrating at night.

B. Our child was asked to get out of the deep pool because she's under 13, even though she is extremely proficient at swimming and we were in the pool with her. Aged based, rather thank skills based. Other hotels have better policies about this.

Aside from this. Great breakfast. Nice coffee shop out the front. The Beach club pool and restaurant were also quite nice too. Once the beach restoration is complete in 12 months time, it'll be a very nice place!
reviews.response:
Dear Valued Guest, 
Thank you for sharing your detailed feedback about your recent stay with us. We are delighted to hear that you enjoyed the breakfast, coffee shop, and our Beach Club pool and restaurant. Your kind words mean a lot to our team.
At the same time, I sincerely apologize for the inconveniences you encountered during your stay. 
I understand how frustrating this must have been, especially at night. We have reported this to our maintenance team for immediate inspection and resolution to prevent such an issue from recurring in the future.
Thank you for your perspective on our age-based pool policy. Our priority is ensuring a safe and enjoyable environment for all guests, and we will explore how we can enhance flexibility without compromising safety.
Thank you once again for choosing to stay with us. We hope to welcome you back in the future and provide an even better experience.

Warm regards,
On behalf of the Hotel Management Team
Alex Linh Dang (Mr)
Assistant Front Office

reviews.title: A hard case to review
reviews.text: The Imperial is grand and chic but old. Therefore the finishing and power points may not be upto standard much for some guests, like my husband, but not so important for others, like me. For me, the one I regretted booking this hotel is the location. If you just want to relax with sun bathing, and swimming, with own and beautiful view pool and beach, then you’d like this. But if you want to mingle with the local and enjoying local foods then this location is not ideal because Thuy Van street is not lively at night anymore. Also my son disappointed with the gym here because he said it’s too simple with not much equipment, for a 5* hotel, and definitely not like the video advertisement. Apart from those, we all love the buffet breakfast, we even surprised that hotel food can have that authentic taste. I also want to praise one staff serving drinks, Ms Lanh, she’s so nice and friendly making my stay a memorable.

reviews.title: Premium double is too small for the room price & range
reviews.text:
-  My booked Premium double room is too small and uncomfortable with a high room price at 5 stars ranking hotel. 
The room only fits 1 double bed, 1 couch, 1 cupboard, 1 desk. No luggage rack. 
( I've stayed in Imperial at hotel residence. The room is better )
- They have a smart TV. Good for entertainment.
- There's a small balcony with Seaview but no chair or tea table so you need to sit on the bed for drinking tea & enjoying Seaview. The Seaview isn't as beautiful as expected.  
- No mirror for dressing or makeup in the bedroom. All the mirrors are just for decoration. 
The bathroom is always wet due to water splashes from the shower & bathtub. I think the bathroom is quite dark. I couldn't see clearly to remove my makeup or to make up.  

+ The best things are 2 beautiful swimming pools even though we can only use the one at the beachside, the other is extremely windy & cold.  The facilities overall are good including the hotel's outer ...  All the staff are nice.

Hy vọng chị đã có nhiều thời gian để tận hưởng các dịch vụ và tiện ích của khách sạn trong thời gian vừa rồi. Đặc biệt là hồ Bơi vườn Thượng Uyển, Hồ bơi và bãi biển riêng tại câu lạc bộ biển Imperial.

Mong gặp lại anh/chị  trong thời gian sớm nhất.
Trân trọng 
Ban Quản Lý Khách Sạn Imperial
